findings.description: _render interpolates msg.subject/to/body directly into raw SMTP headers with CRLF, allowing header injection (extra To/Bcc/From headers or body smuggling) from attacker-controlled input.
msg.to is passed unvalidated to sendmail as the envelope recipient, enabling SMTP command/recipient injection and unintended fan-out if 'to' contains list separators or CRLF.
SMTP connection on port 587 never calls starttls(), so message bodies (and any future credentials) are transmitted in cleartext.
Dedup key is only msg.to, so distinct messages (different subject/body) to the same recipient are silently skipped as duplicates.
flush clears _pending but not _seen, so any recipient ever queued is permanently blocked from future enqueue until clear_dedup_cache is called manually.
flush opens and tears down a new SMTP connection for every message instead of reusing one connection, multiplying connection/handshake overhead.
If sendmail/quit raises mid-loop the exception propagates and _pending is never cleared, so already-sent messages remain and will be re-sent on a retry, causing duplicate delivery.
No timeout passed to smtplib.SMTP, so a hung server can block flush indefinitely.
_seen grows without bound across the queue's lifetime since entries are never evicted, a slow memory leak for long-lived queues.
Message.template field is declared but never read anywhere (e.g., not used in _render), so templating is effectively dead/unimplemented.
'field' is imported from dataclasses but never used.
MIMEText is imported but never used; rendering is done with manual string concatenation instead.
preview uses '\n' line endings while _render uses '\r\n', so preview does not faithfully represent the wire format.
